title: RoboForex Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of RoboForex pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
RoboForex Pricing Overview

RoboForex operates on a zero monthly account fee model across all account types. Whether you open a Pro account, ECN account, or their proprietary R StocksTrader platform, there's no subscription cost. The broker generates revenue through spreads (the bid-ask difference) and commissions on ECN accounts, rather than monthly charges. With a $10 minimum deposit, RoboForex is among the most accessible forex brokers globally for traders starting with minimal capital.

This "free account, pay through trading" model makes RoboForex attractive to beginner traders and capital-constrained traders, though you'll need to understand the total cost of trading beyond the account fee itself.

All RoboForex Plans Compared

RoboForex offers four primary account types, each zero-cost to open and maintain:

Account Type Monthly Fee Platforms Best For Min Deposit
Pro / ProCent $0 MT4, MT5, Mobile New traders, all asset classes $10
ECN $0 MT4, MT5, cTrader Scalpers, high-volume traders $10
Prime $0 MT4, MT5 Low-cost traders, copy trading $10
R StocksTrader $0 Proprietary Platform Stock traders, global equities $10

Pro / ProCent Account: RoboForex's standard offering for forex, metals, and indices. Competitive fixed spreads make this suitable for most beginner traders. The Pro account uses standard pricing models and is available across MT4, MT5, and mobile platforms.

ECN Account: Targets professional scalpers and high-frequency traders. ECN accounts offer raw spreads starting from 0 pips on major currency pairs with a $4 per lot commission. This is ideal if you trade frequently and want the tightest possible execution costs. Available on MT4, MT5, and cTrader platforms.

Prime Account: A middle-ground option with fixed spreads and no commissions—useful for traders who want predictable costs without per-lot charges. Integrated with CopyFX, RoboForex's copy trading platform, so passive traders can mirror professional signal providers automatically.

R StocksTrader: RoboForex's proprietary platform designed specifically for stock and ETF trading. Offers access to 12,000+ global tradable assets including US equities, European stocks, and emerging market shares. Zero commissions on stock orders with fixed spreads.

Free Plan / Free Trial

RoboForex doesn't charge monthly account fees—all accounts are functionally "free" to open and maintain. However, this differs from a true free trial; you're not getting a free trading balance.

What's included in the "free" account:

  • Full access to all trading platforms (MT4, MT5, cTrader, R StocksTrader)
  • Real-time charts, technical analysis tools, and trading alerts
  • Mobile trading apps for iOS and Android
  • API access for automated trading and algorithmic strategies
  • Educational resources and webinars
  • Risk management tools including stop-losses and take-profit orders

What you still need: The minimum deposit of $10 to start trading. This is a real deposit—not a practice account balance. If you want to practice risk-free, you'll need to use a demo account instead, which RoboForex offers separately with simulated funds.

For complete beginners, RoboForex's demo account is worth testing before committing real capital. It gives you full platform access with virtual money to learn execution, platform features, and test strategies.

Hidden Costs and Fees

While there are no monthly subscription fees, RoboForex's actual cost structure includes several other charges:

Spreads (all accounts): The primary cost. Pro and Prime accounts have fixed spreads that vary by instrument—forex pairs typically start at 1-3 pips depending on volatility. ECN accounts have raw (floating) spreads averaging 0-1 pip, but with the $4 per lot commission added on top.

ECN Commission: $4 per lot traded on ECN accounts. For a standard 100,000-unit lot, this equals $4 per trade ($2 per round-trip). High-frequency scalpers accumulate these quickly; a trader executing 20 scalps per day would pay $160/day in commissions.

Inactivity fees: RoboForex charges fees on accounts inactive for 12+ months. While not explicitly listed in the data provided, this is a common policy among multi-asset brokers. Check the current fee schedule on their website.

Withdrawal fees: May apply depending on payment method. Bank transfers, credit cards, and e-wallets (Skrill, Neteller) may have associated withdrawal charges—typically $10-50 for slower methods.

Overnight holding costs (swaps): If you hold forex positions overnight, you'll pay or earn swap fees depending on interest rate differentials between currency pairs. These are not flat fees but variable daily charges/credits.

Data feed costs: Real-time market data is free; delayed quotes require no subscription. Premium news and economic calendars are included at no extra cost.

No platform fees: MT4, MT5, cTrader, and R StocksTrader access are all included. No per-platform charges.

RoboForex Pricing vs Competitors

How does RoboForex stack up against other major forex and multi-asset brokers?

Broker Account Fee Min Deposit Avg Spread (EUR/USD) Best For
RoboForex $0 $10 1.5-2 pips (Pro); 0 pips (ECN) Beginners, scalpers, copy traders
Interactive Brokers $0 (minimum activity $0) $0 (tier-dependent) 0.2-0.5 pips + commission Professional, diversified traders
Oanda $0 $1 1-2 pips Retail traders, educational
Pepperstone $0 $200 0.1-0.5 pips (raw) Professional scalpers

RoboForex's competitive edge: The $10 minimum deposit is exceptionally low—most competitors require $100-$500 minimums. The ECN 0-pip spreads are competitive with professional-grade brokers like Pepperstone, though the $4 per lot commission adds up for high-frequency traders.

Where RoboForex costs more: If you scalp frequently, you'll pay more in commissions than at a fixed-spread broker. Interactive Brokers offers tighter professional spreads (0.2-0.5 pips) if you qualify for their account minimums.

Where RoboForex wins: Capital-constrained traders and copy traders find RoboForex unbeatable. The combination of low deposits, multiple platforms, and integrated CopyFX is rare.

Is RoboForex Worth the Price?

For new traders with limited capital (under $500): RoboForex is excellent value. The $10 deposit lets you learn with real money without overcommitting. Fixed spreads on Pro accounts are transparent and reasonable. Rating: 9/10 value.

For passive copy traders: Exceptional value. CopyFX is free to use, spreads are fair, and you can start with $10. No better option if you want to replicate professional traders automatically. Rating: 9.5/10 value.

For active forex scalpers: Good but not optimal. ECN 0-pip spreads are competitive, but $4 per lot commissions add 0.4 pips to your cost on a 10-lot scalp. You'd need to generate consistent pips above this commission to profit. Compare with fixed-spread brokers if you trade fewer than 10 lots per position. Rating: 7/10 value.

For stock traders: Good value. The R StocksTrader platform offers 12,000+ assets with zero commissions and reasonable spreads. Better suited for swing traders than high-frequency equity traders. Rating: 8/10 value.

When to upgrade account types: Stay on Pro until you're consistently profitable and trading more than 50 lots per week. Then migrate to ECN if you want raw spreads. Most retail traders never need to switch.

How to Save on RoboForex

Discount codes and promotions: RoboForex periodically offers deposit bonuses (typically 30-50% up to $5,000) for new accounts. Check their promotions page or affiliate partner sites for active codes. Note: bonus funds are often locked until you meet trading volume requirements.

Cashback programs: Some affiliate networks offer 20-50% cashback on spreads and commissions. If you trade multiple lots per week, this can offset 10-30% of your total trading costs.

Use CopyFX strategically: If you use CopyFX, you pay no extra fees to mirror professional traders. This can reduce your overall time and decision-making errors, offsetting higher spreads through better performance.

Avoid the inactivity fee: Make at least one trade or transfer every 12 months to avoid dormancy charges. Even a single micro-lot trade counts.

Consolidate positions: If you trade multiple small lots, consolidate to fewer larger lots to reduce per-lot commission drag on ECN accounts. A 10-lot trade costs $40 total; ten 1-lot trades cost $40 but are less efficient.

No annual discount: RoboForex doesn't offer annual prepayment discounts because there are no account fees. Your only savings come through lower trading volumes and promotional bonuses.

Bottom line: RoboForex's $0 account fee and $10 minimum deposit make it one of the most accessible brokers globally. For beginners and copy traders, the value is exceptional. Professional scalpers should run the math on ECN commissions vs. fixed spreads elsewhere. Overall rating: 4.1/5—a solid choice for the right trader profile.
